etymology.description: 角(角) (jiǎo, "horn") + 刀(刀) (dāo, "knife") + 牛(牛) (niú, "cow") -> a knife separates a horn from a cow -> cut up an ox
etymology.interpretation: The character 解(解) (jiě, "untie") combines 角(角) (jiǎo, "horn"), 刀(刀) (dāo, "knife"), and 牛(牛) (niú, "cow") to illustrate the action of cutting a horn off a cow. This imagery suggests the act of separating or dismantling, leading to the sense of 'to untie' or 'to disassemble.'

examples.chinese: 他需要解开这个复杂的结(他需要解開這個複雜的結)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xūyào jiěkāi zhège fùzá de jié.
examples.english: He needs to untie this complex knot.

examples.chinese: 我们应该找到解决问题的方法(我們應該找到解決問題的方法)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi zhǎodào jiějué wèntí de fāngfǎ.
examples.english: We should find a way to solve the problem.

examples.chinese: 他终于解出了这个难题(他終於解出了這個難題)。
examples.english: He finally untied the difficult puzzle.
